Key features and workflows for finance & accounting teams
How Notion fits into the AI-powered productivity stack

slides.title: Vibe-Coded Desk Reservation Tool — Inspiring Others to Build
slides.notes: Brock Beyer built a desk reservation tool 100% vibe-coded — great story about a non-developer just going for it with AI. Includes a helpful prompt guide at the bottom. The angle: inspiring others in finance & accounting to start building their own tools.

slides.title: AI Can't Do Transfer Pricing?
slides.notes: Secret CFO post — PSA on why you shouldn't hack transfer pricing with AI. Transfer pricing is one of those areas where AI oversimplification could create serious compliance and regulatory risk.

slides.title: Replit Controller Builds Accounting Close Tool
slides.notes: Mohamed Alsabawi built a custom accounting close tool using Replit's controller agent. Shows how non-engineers can now build real accounting workflow tools with AI-powered development platforms.

slides.title: The Death of Apps & Rise of the Meta-App
slides.notes: Zach Lloyd argues meta-apps like Claude Code, Codex, and Warp bypass traditional apps entirely — you start with what you want to accomplish and the meta-app goes right to the solution. MCP, CLIs, and APIs are just a stopgap because data is siloed across legacy apps. Connects to Ore's EP1 take 'The UI is Dead' — this extends the thesis from UI dying to entire apps being replaced.

segments.name: One Thing I Did with AI

slides.title: What Is an Agent?
bullets: Breaking down AI agents — what they are, how they work, and why everyone's talking about them
Recurring segment: demystify AI concepts for finance & accounting professionals
slides.notes: Explainer segment — make AI jargon accessible. First topic: What is an agent? Cover autonomous action, tool use, reasoning loops, and real-world examples in finance.

slides.title: Uncookme!
bullets: Recurring segment: invite a guest who has a real problem
Hosts live-solve the problem on-air using AI tools
Part troubleshooting, part showcase, all entertainment
slides.notes: Format: Guest presents their problem → hosts brainstorm and build a solution live → guest reacts. Think live consulting meets improv.

segments.name: Useful Things We Saw This Week

slides.title: BigCo Corner
bullets: Recurring segment: spotlight how big companies are adopting and deploying AI
Enterprise AI strategy, internal tools, workforce changes, and real implementations
slides.notes: Discussion segment covering AI moves by large corporations — think Big 4, Fortune 500, major banks, tech giants. What are they building, buying, and rolling out?

slides.title: AI Agents Using Crypto for Payments
slides.notes: AI agents are starting to use crypto rails for payments — the intersection of autonomous agents and decentralized finance. How crypto solves the payments problem for AI agents that need to transact without human intervention.

slides.title: Crypto Wasn't Made for Humans — It Was Made for AI
bullets: Ever eyeballed a crypto address? 0x7a250d... — humans weren't built for this
Protocol gets hacked → you get notified on Twitter. What if you're not on Twitter? AI agents monitor 24/7
Like SWIFT codes, crypto addresses are machine-native — AI reads them natively
Example: You want safer DeFi → AI agent moves your funds from a risky protocol into AAVE, or presents you a plan to approve
You may not even know what AAVE is — and that's the point. AI abstracts the complexity
slides.notes: Source: Bankless — https://x.com/bankless/status/2028455291000340911 — Thesis: Crypto's UX was never designed for humans. Addresses, protocols, monitoring — it's all machine-readable. AI agents are the missing UX layer that makes crypto actually usable. Recurring segment potential: exploring the AI × crypto intersection.

slides.title: The Future Accountant
bullets: Welcome guest accountant Thea Yatras
Recurring segment: exploring what the modern accountant looks like in the age of AI
slides.notes: Guest segment featuring accountant Thea Yatras. Discussion on how AI is reshaping the accounting profession — skills, tools, workflows, and what the future accountant needs to thrive.

slides.title: What Really Happens When You Send a Chat Message?
bullets: Deep-dive: trace what actually happens under the hood when you send a message to an LLM
Actually ask an LLM live on the call to explain it — meta moment
Reference: Anthropic engineering on code execution with MCP
slides.notes: Recurring segment: go absurdly deep on a technical topic and make it entertaining. First topic: the journey of a chat message — from keystroke to tokenization to inference to response. Actually demo it live by asking an LLM to explain itself. Source: https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/code-execution-with-mcp
